title: Map the reservation lifecycle
description: bookit reservations move through a lifecycle of statuses, and the team needs an accurate map of that lifecycle as it is actually implemented - not as the documentation describes it. Produce a written report that lists every reservation status, every permitted status transition together with what triggers it (an HTTP endpoint, an internal code path, or something else), and every rejected transition together with where the rejection is enforced. Pay particular attention to any transition that happens without a dedicated endpoint being called. Every claim must cite file and line in the target repository at the starting revision.
acceptance_criteria: The report lists every reservation status defined in the code, with a file:line citation for each definition.
The report lists every status transition the code permits and names the trigger of each (HTTP endpoint, internal code path, or other).
Transitions that occur without a dedicated endpoint call are identified and their trigger conditions are explained.
The report states which transitions are rejected and cites the code that makes that decision.
The report notes where each transition is recorded for audit purposes.
Every claim carries a file:line citation that is valid at the starting revision.
No file in the target repository is modified.
constraints: Read-only task: observation capabilities only, no edits to the target repository.
Citations must refer to the starting revision, not to a modified working tree.
non_goals: Fixing any defect noticed during the investigation.
Correcting documentation that disagrees with the code (report the disagreement instead).
Describing the cancellation fee schedule beyond where fees attach to transitions.

visible_validation.description: The lifecycle report exists and every claim has a file:line citation.
The target repository working tree is unchanged (empty output).

expected_artifacts: reports/bk-001-lifecycle.md: the lifecycle map with file:line citations
Execution trace of the investigation run

human_review_rubric.criterion: All statuses and transitions found, including those not triggered by a dedicated endpoint
Citations spot-check correctly against the starting revision
Investigation used a sensible number of reads and searches rather than exhaustive file dumps

known_ambiguities: The required depth for audit-trail side effects is unspecified: naming where transitions are recorded is required, describing the audit schema is not.
Report format is free-form as long as statuses, transitions, triggers, and citations are unambiguous.
expected_failure_modes: Missing transitions that are triggered internally rather than by an endpoint.
Citing documentation instead of code as evidence.
